title: Explainer: frequentist vs Bayesian reading of a result (intro)
context: Bayesian adaptive trial designs, Bayesian posterior probability statements, and hybrid Bayesian-frequentist reporting are increasingly appearing in oncology literature (especially in basket trials, adaptive platform trials, and regulatory submissions). Clinicians trained in frequentist reasoning need a plain-language introduction to what a Bayesian posterior probability means and how to read it, without this explainer becoming a full statistics course. Scope-guarding is critical: the goal is recognition and correct interpretation, not fluency in Bayesian computation. This is a backlog item that should not proceed if it risks becoming too dense for a general clinician audience.
objective: Author, biostatistician-sign-off, and publish an introductory explainer on the frequentist vs Bayesian reading of a trial result — covering the core conceptual difference, how to read a posterior probability statement, and common traps — with scope-guarding against becoming a full statistics course.
acceptanceCriteria: The explainer provides plain and precise definitions of the frequentist p-value (probability of data given H0) and the Bayesian posterior probability (probability of hypothesis given data + prior), with the conceptual difference stated clearly.

An explicit commonTrap section covers: misreading a Bayesian posterior probability as a p-value equivalent; assuming a 'probability of success' from a Bayesian adaptive trial is the same as a frequentist significance test.

The explainer stays at the level of recognition and correct interpretation, not Bayesian computation; a scope-guard note reminds authors this is an introduction, not a course — if the draft exceeds a defined length or requires prior elicitation to explain, it must be scoped back.

A simple worked example (e.g. a reported posterior probability from a published open-access Bayesian adaptive trial) is used to illustrate the reading; all figures and numbers emitted by committed script and diff-matched by repro-005.
Every conceptual claim cites an authoritative methods source; howToReadInAPaper checklist included.
Structural 'educational, not clinical advice' framing present.
Biostatistician statsSignoff recorded; clinical-framing and accessibility reviews passed.
Zero stated statistical falsehoods; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; DCO signed-off.
resources: Methods literature on Bayesian clinical trials (cited per-claim); FDA guidance on Bayesian adaptive trial design
output: A published, biostatistician-signed-off introductory Bayesian vs frequentist explainer with 100% reproducible worked example, full provenance, all claims cited, accessibility passed, structural 'not clinical advice' frame, and explicit scope-guard that keeps it at an introductory level.
